rule: Open with a what-if or contrarian hook — never a hashtag or news announcement
reason: What-if hooks (Code to Figma: 22.4K) and identity hooks (India's answer: 19K) massively outperform news hooks (UPI for Agents: 5.2K).

rule: Target 31–36 seconds. Never go over 40.
reason: All top performers sit in this range. Attention drops after 36s. JioFrames at 41s is a risk.

rule: Chase shares and saves, not likes
reason: Code to Figma had 406 shares + 435 saves — near equal to likes. Utility reels that people send or bookmark are the best growth lever.

rule: Use the India vs West frame whenever possible
reason: Two of top 3 reels use this angle. Indian audience responds strongly to 'we built our own version' narratives.

rule: Lead with a strong product or screen visual — no plain logo graphics
reason: UPI reel used a logo graphic only. All stronger reels had product shots or screen recordings as the visual hook.

rule: Use triple short sentences for rhythm at least once per script
reason: e.g. 'No studio. No photographer. No budget.' — creates punch and makes it feel spoken, not written.

rule: Always end with a debate-inviting question, not a statement
reason: Open-ended questions drive comments. Statements close the loop. Keep it open.

rule: Avoid fintech, infra, and policy topics unless tied to a design or product angle
reason: UPI for Agents (5.2K) shows niche infra topics underperform. Rahul's audience is designers + AI curious, not fintech.

rule: No intro. Zero. Straight into the hook from frame 1.
reason: Every top reel opens cold. 'Hey guys' or any warm-up kills retention in the first 2 seconds.

rule: Drop one specific technical detail per script for credibility
reason: Naming the exact tool, model, price, or spec (e.g. '₹10,000', 'Nano Banana model') signals authority and stops vague generalism.

rule: Prioritize AI design tools, AI hardware, and India tech narratives as topics
reason: Top 3 reels all sit in these categories. This is Rahul's lane and his audience's interest zone.

rule: Rate every topic before scripting — only write scripts for topics scoring 7+/10
reason: Not every tweet is reel-worthy. Rating filters effort and keeps quality high.

rule: Use punchy, opinionated language — never soft or hedged
reason: 'Not generic AI slop' outperforms 'may not look great'. Rahul's audience responds to confidence, not caveats.

rule: Script first, audio second, video third — never skip approval gates
reason: Each phase costs time and money. Catching issues at script stage is cheapest.

rule: Always pull source context via X API before writing — never script from memory
reason: Fresh data = accurate scripts = credibility. Hallucinated stats destroy trust.

rule: B-roll links must be attached before sending to Peeyush
reason: Editor needs visual references to cut to. No b-roll = slower edit = delayed post.

rule: High shares-to-views ratio = strong reel. Target >1.5% share rate.
reason: Code to Figma: 1.8% share rate. Google 3D print: 3.3% share rate. Both outperformed on distribution.

rule: Ride trending topics within 24–48h of breaking — speed is distribution
reason: AI news cycles fast. A reel on day 1 of a launch gets algorithm boost. Day 5 is too late.

rule: Profile activity metric = topic-audience fit. Low profile activity = wrong audience reached.
reason: UPI reel: 10 profile visits. Code to Figma: 159. Right topic pulls the right followers.

rule: Every 10 reels, review rules and update — kill what's stale, add what's new
reason: Algorithms shift, audience evolves. Rules are living documents, not commandments.
